summary: Parallel review: FAIL (3 critical, 11 warning, 11 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:78` — `# SKIP` directive not excluded; `not ok N - test # SKIP reason` produces `findings.critical++` when it should produce 0; extend guard to `!/# (?:TODO|SKIP)\b/i` and add a test
🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:1` — no callers in `bin/`; module is inert in production; confirm integration task is tracked before marking feature shippable

The stated requirement is cleanly met. `parseTap` follows the registry pattern perfectly — correct 3-arg signature, bare function reference in PARSERS, uniform return shape `{ findings, summary, meta: { messages } }`. Task-1's architectural work paid off: the extension was two lines plus a function. The one real gap (`# SKIP` not excluded) is a TAP spec compliance issue that will cause false positives in production against `node:test` output, but it's a secondary edge case not in the stated acceptance criteria. It goes to backlog as a 🟡, not a blocker.
🟡 bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:78 — `not ok … # SKIP` counted as critical; TAP spec defines SKIP as an intentional skip, not a failure. Change guard to `!/# (?:TODO|SKIP)\b/i` and add a test

🟡 test/validator-parsers.test.mjs:19 — `parseJunitXml(xml)` called with 1 arg after signature became 3-arg in task-1; update all JUnit XML call sites to `(xml, "", 0)` to accurately reflect the interface contract

The core feature is correct — `parseTap` properly detects `not ok` lines, excludes `# TODO` directives, counts each as one critical finding, and is wired into the registry cleanly. The two 🟡 warnings are real gaps: the `# SKIP` exclusion is a TAP spec correctness hole (false positives for intentionally-skipped tests), and the 1-arg JUnit XML test calls are a contract accuracy issue left over from task-1. Neither blocks merge by themselves, but both should go to backlog.

🟡 `.team/features/external-validator-integration/tasks/task-2/handshake.json` — Gate output is truncated before the `validator-parsers.test.mjs` block; claimed 553-test pass count has no direct evidence in the provided gate output; save `test-output.txt` as a handshake artifact (same gap flagged but never resolved across task-1 run_1–run_3)
The core requirement is met. The 🟡 finding is a process/evidence gap, not a behavioral failure. **No 🔴 blockers.**

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:78` — `# SKIP` directive not excluded; `not ok X # SKIP reason` is a TAP-spec skip (not a failure) but produces one critical finding under current logic; add `!/# SKIP\b/i` guard alongside the existing `# TODO` guard and add a matching test

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:79` — verbatim TAP lines (attacker-controlled stdout) pushed to `messages[]` without stripping control characters; `not ok 1 - \x1b[2J...` injects terminal escape sequences into CLI output; strip `/[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g` before `messages.push(trimmed)` (same fix as JUnit parser line 49 — now affects both parsers)

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:78` — `# SKIP` TAP directive not excluded; `not ok 3 - test name # SKIP reason` is a TAP-spec intentional skip but produces one critical finding (false positive); extend the exclusion to `/# (?:TODO|SKIP)\b/i` and add a corresponding test

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:84` — non-empty non-TAP stdout produces `critical=0` indistinguishable from a clean run; a misconfigured validator silently passes the gate once wired in; add `meta.parseWarning = true` when stdout is non-empty and no TAP structure is detected (carried from JUnit security reviews run_2/run_3)
- The ANSI injection and `meta.parseWarning` gaps are carried from JUnit and now affect `parseTap` identically; they remain 🟡 (not 🔴) because the module is still inert in production.
